repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with several existing research threads
user_request: I just had an idea about using verifier disagreement as a confidence signal. Capture it and figure out where it belongs.
expected_result: Lab checks existing nodes and Paper Wiki related ideas before placing or creating an idea node.
must_not_do: Do not leave the idea only in chat.
Do not create a duplicate node without graph lookup.
Do not treat the idea as a Paper Wiki source fact.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active and paused research directions
user_request: I want to continue the long-horizon goal stability direction. Help me recover the right node and next step.
expected_result: Lab finds the matching node, checks Paper Wiki prior, restores state, and proposes the next research action.
must_not_do: Do not start a new thread before node lookup.
Do not answer from chat memory only.
Do not start coding before recovering node context.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active node and experiment history
user_request: I forgot why the active node was designed this way. Recover the context before we continue.
expected_result: Lab reads active node history and Paper Wiki grounding, then summarizes decisions, evidence, open questions, and next action.
must_not_do: Do not create new state when recovery is enough.
Do not skip node history.
Do not invent prior without wiki context.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with an active experiment node
user_request: I finished the probe for the active node. The effect only appears after 8k tokens. Put this in the right place.
expected_result: Lab records local experiment evidence against the active node and updates next checks without making it a source fact.
must_not_do: Do not leave the result only in chat.
Do not create a new node before checking active state.
Do not publish local run results as wiki facts.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with conflicting experiment records
user_request: Two runs disagree about whether the mechanism helps. Reconcile the evidence, check Paper Wiki prior for variance or failure modes, and update the node state.
expected_result: Lab compares evidence, checks prior for variance or failure modes, and updates confidence/state plus follow-up criteria.
must_not_do: Do not pick a winner without evidence analysis.
Do not skip Paper Wiki prior.
Do not rerun tests inside Lab.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with an evaluation node
user_request: What baseline should this experiment compare against so the result is fair?
expected_result: Lab finds the eval node, checks related papers for baselines, and records baseline rationale and limitations.
must_not_do: Do not choose a baseline from memory only.
Do not treat baseline choice as ordinary coding.
Do not mutate source facts.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active hypothesis node
user_request: Design the evaluation protocol for this hypothesis before we implement anything.
expected_result: Lab finds the hypothesis node, checks Paper Wiki eval protocols, defines metrics and evidence identity, and updates the node before coding.
must_not_do: Do not start coding first.
Do not skip node lookup.
Do not leave evidence return criteria unspecified.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with a proposed idea node
user_request: Before I invest in this idea, check related work risk and whether someone has already done it.
expected_result: Lab finds or creates the candidate node, checks Paper Wiki for related papers and ideas, and records related-work risk.
must_not_do: Do not do broad guessing instead of wiki grounding.
Do not skip graph placement.
Do not commit to implementation before risk review.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active idea node
user_request: Use the wiki papers to judge whether the active idea is feasible or already contradicted.
expected_result: Lab finds the active idea node, checks Paper Wiki papers and contradictions, and records feasibility plus open questions.
must_not_do: Do not answer without node lookup.
Do not rewrite wiki pages directly.
Do not treat feasibility as final source fact.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with local findings and linked wiki context
user_request: This result might be a real local finding. Decide whether it should attach to the node or become a wiki proposal later.
expected_result: Lab attaches the finding to the node or prepares a proposal boundary, keeping local evidence separate from source facts.
must_not_do: Do not publish directly to canonical wiki.
Do not drop node attachment.
Do not blur local evidence with source facts.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active implementation-ready probe node
user_request: Implement the next probe for the active node, but first make sure it matches the relevant papers.
expected_result: Lab checks the node, Paper Wiki, and open-source implementation hints, then emits Research Grounding Injection for coding.
must_not_do: Do not bypass Lab because implementation is mentioned.
Do not implement inside Lab.
Do not omit evidence return criteria.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with recent failed experiment evidence
user_request: The latest run regressed after changing metric aggregation. Decide if this is an invalid eval and prepare debugging work.
expected_result: Lab interprets the failure against node evidence and prior, then injects concrete debug grounding into normal coding.
must_not_do: Do not debug immediately without evidence identity.
Do not treat local failure as a source fact.
Do not edit code inside Lab.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with coding task tied to a method node
user_request: Before coding this routing heuristic, check whether our wiki has papers that implement a similar method and whether their code is public.
expected_result: Lab checks similar method papers and public code references, then summarizes implementation prior for coding.
must_not_do: Do not code before checking wiki papers.
Do not skip open-source implementation grounding.
Do not route directly to wiki as the primary entry.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active probe node
user_request: Define the smallest implementation scope for this probe, check related paper/code prior, and prepare the Research Grounding Injection for the coding workflow.
expected_result: Lab uses node identity and paper/code prior to define minimal implementation scope, then emits Research Grounding Injection for coding.
must_not_do: Do not start implementation inside Lab.
Do not expand into a general framework.
Do not skip code grounding.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active experiment plan
user_request: Before coding starts, define what evidence the implementation should return so the result is useful for the research node.
expected_result: Lab defines metrics, command/config identity, expected outputs, validity criteria, and return signal in a Research Grounding Injection.
must_not_do: Do not start coding without evidence contract.
Do not skip node lookup.
Do not record coding logs as source facts.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with recent experiment evidence
user_request: The result got worse after the attention-mask change. Decide whether this is a bug or research signal, then prepare code-debug grounding for the checks the coding workflow should run.
expected_result: Lab checks evidence and failure-mode prior, then emits Research Grounding Injection for concrete implementation checks.
must_not_do: Do not debug code immediately without Lab evidence identity.
Do not skip experiment history.
Do not publish unverified local failure as wiki fact.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active mechanism hypothesis
user_request: For this probe, identify the intermediate activations or counters to observe, then prepare the Research Grounding Injection so the coding workflow can implement them.
expected_result: Lab finds the mechanism node, checks probing papers and code examples, and injects observation targets for implementation.
must_not_do: Do not invent observations without prior.
Do not implement inside Lab.
Do not lose hypothesis identity.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with candidate experiment node
user_request: Build the first benchmark harness for this idea after checking what related papers measured.
expected_result: Lab grounds benchmark choice and available implementations before handing harness work to normal coding.
must_not_do: Do not choose benchmarks from memory only.
Do not code benchmark harness inside Lab.
Do not omit limitation notes.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with suspicious benchmark gain
user_request: The benchmark gain looks suspicious. Prepare the diagnostic reruns we need to rule out leakage.
expected_result: Lab checks eval node and leakage prior, then injects diagnostic rerun commands, validity criteria, and return signal.
must_not_do: Do not accept the gain as evidence immediately.
Do not debug without eval node context.
Do not skip leakage prior.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active adaptation node
user_request: Adapt the mechanism from the related paper into our probe. First check how their repo implemented it.
expected_result: Lab checks the adaptation node, related paper, and public implementation before injecting coding context.
must_not_do: Do not adapt from memory.
Do not skip code/repo links.
Do not implement inside Lab.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active ablation node
user_request: Implement the ablation for this node, but first check the related paper and its public code so the coding workflow has the right grounding.
expected_result: Lab checks node identity, related paper, public code, evidence contract, and emits Research Grounding Injection for normal coding.
must_not_do: Do not implement inside Lab.
Do not skip code/repo links.
Do not lose the ablation node identity.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active failure-analysis node
user_request: Add the diagnostic counter that will distinguish bug from research signal; prepare the Research Grounding Injection before coding.
expected_result: Lab checks evidence, Paper Wiki failure-mode prior, code-level implementation hints, and emits Research Grounding Injection for coding.
must_not_do: Do not debug without Lab evidence identity.
Do not skip implementation prior.
Do not record coding output as source fact.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ but the requested edit has no research meaning
user_request: Rename this local helper variable from tmp to candidate_count.
expected_result: Skip Lab and handle the mechanical code edit normally.
must_not_do: Do not create Lab state.
Do not retrieve Paper Wiki context.
Do not emit Research Grounding Injection.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ but task is documentation formatting
user_request: Fix the Markdown table alignment in README.
expected_result: Skip Lab and do ordinary documentation cleanup.
must_not_do: Do not check research graph.
Do not check Paper Wiki.
Do not attach this to an experiment node.

repo_state: repo may or may not have .meridian/; user asks for Meridian readiness
user_request: Check whether Meridian plugin, MCP, and local workspace setup are ready.
expected_result: Use Meridian setup/status, not Lab research workflow.
must_not_do: Do not run a Lab research task.
Do not retrieve papers.
Do not emit coding grounding.

repo_state: Paper Wiki workspace is configured; no Lab node is referenced
user_request: What papers in my wiki are relevant to long-running agent goal stability?
expected_result: Use the Paper Wiki retrieval workflow directly without Lab state mutation.
must_not_do: Do not create a Lab node.
Do not require .meridian.
Do not treat retrieval as coding preflight.

repo_state: Paper Wiki workspace is configured; user is adding a source

expected_result: Use Update Wiki ingest/publish boundaries, not Lab.
must_not_do: Do not create Lab state.
Do not emit Research Grounding Injection.
Do not present debug drafts as product output.

repo_state: repo has no .meridian/ and the task is ordinary bug fixing
user_request: Fix the failing unit test for this parser edge case.
expected_result: Use normal coding workflow because there is no initialized Lab state or research intent.
must_not_do: Do not invent Lab state.
Do not retrieve wiki context.
Do not block on Meridian setup.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ but user asks for package release mechanics
user_request: Publish this version and update the local installed package following the README flow.
expected_result: Use normal release workflow; Lab should not own publishing mechanics.
must_not_do: Do not create experiment evidence.
Do not check research graph.
Do not turn release work into a Lab node.

user_request: MCP tools are not callable again. Diagnose and repair the configuration.
expected_result: Use Meridian setup diagnostics and repair flow, not Lab.
must_not_do: Do not route to Paper Wiki retrieval.
Do not create Lab state.
Do not emit coding grounding.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ but refactor is mechanical and behavior-preserving
user_request: Extract this repeated path normalization code into a helper without changing behavior.
expected_result: Use normal coding workflow unless the user ties the refactor to a research node.
must_not_do: Do not check Paper Wiki.
Do not create a research node.
Do not emit Research Grounding Injection.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ but request is about Codex usage
